app.title: Mood Tracking App
app.subtitle: Track how you feel over time with a simple 1–5 mood scale

mood.title: Mood Tracker
mood.description: Select how you're feeling and then submit. Date and time are recorded automatically.
mood.notePlaceholder: Add an optional note about how you're feeling...
mood.noMoods: No moods logged yet. Choose how you feel to get started.

exercise.title: Exercise Tracker
exercise.description: Log your workouts to keep track of your physical activity.

food.title: Food Tracker
food.description: Log your meals and snacks to keep track of your nutrition.

sleep.title: Sleep Tracker
sleep.description: Log your sleep duration and quality.

dailyGraph.title: Today's Mood
dailyGraph.noData: No moods logged for today yet. Record how you feel to see it here.

history.title: History
history.noHistory: No history yet. Start logging your mood to see it here.

dataManager.title: Data Management
dataManager.autoSaveDesc: Data is automatically saved to a file whenever you make changes.
dataManager.noFileDesc: File will be set up automatically on first use. Your data is saved to browser storage.
dataManager.manualExportDesc: Download your data as a JSON file that you can edit directly
dataManager.importDesc: Load data from a JSON file or paste JSON directly
dataManager.clearConfirm: Are you sure you want to clear all data? This cannot be undone.
dataManager.errorInvalidData: Invalid data format. Please check your JSON structure.
dataManager.formatDesc: Your JSON file should follow this structure:

suggestions.title: Lifestyle Suggestions
suggestions.generic: Take a deep breath. Try spending 5 minutes meditating or listening to calming music.
suggestions.exerciseMore: You haven't been exercising much recently. Try going for a short walk! Physical activity can help improve your mood.
suggestions.exerciseLess: You've been exercising a lot lately! Make sure to take time to rest and recover. Overtraining can sometimes lead to fatigue and lower mood.
suggestions.eatMore: Your recorded calorie intake has been somewhat low. Remember that eating regular, energizing meals can have a big impact on how you feel.
suggestions.eatLess: Your recorded calorie intake is a bit high. While treating yourself is great, sometimes heavier meals can leave you feeling sluggish. Try balancing with lighter options.
suggestions.disclaimer: This is provided for entertainment purposes. We are not health professionals, and this is not medical advice.
